extractor: Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code, session-per-pass workflow v5.0.0)

project_metadata.paper_title: Reliability in research with large language models is a property of the human–AI system
project_metadata.research_context: Argues that reliability in research use of large language models is a property of the human–AI system rather than the model alone, grounded in two episodes: a 2025 campaign applying commercial LLMs to an archaeological study of research-software longevity (four stages, ~2,000 verified outcomes) and a 2026 re-application through a registry-grounded literature-search agent. Translates the observed persistence of synthesis-boundary confabulation into design principles for researcher-built scaffolding.

session_log: Session A (2026-07-24): Pre-Flight + Pass 0 (metadata) + Pass 6 (infrastructure, light mode: FAIR scoring deferred by design for pre-submission draft)
extraction_notes.extraction_strategy: Session-per-pass workflow v5.0.0. Purpose: pre-submission quality check (claims vs evidence, RDMAP). FAIR assessment and Cluster 3 (computational reproducibility) deferred: pre-submission, repositories and DOIs not final.

source_files.pdf: input/sources/original-pdf/Ross - 2026 - Reliability in research with LLMs is a property of the human-AI system (draft 2026-07-24).pdf

known_limitations: Pre-submission draft: infrastructure sections (funding, data availability, ORCIDs, author contributions) largely absent by design, not as a transparency failure
Markdown conversion flattened tables into linear text (10 numbered tables present as prose); page numbers resolved against the PDF during extraction
Section-header detection produced artefact headings in the results tables and references region of the markdown; extraction uses the true section structure from the PDF

persistent_identifiers.notes: None present. Expected for a pre-submission draft; ORCIDs and DOIs are normally added at submission/publication.

funding.notes: No funding or acknowledgements section in the draft.

data_availability.notes: No formal data availability statement yet. Section 5.3 commits to 'Provision of all outputs, specifications, and session transcripts (Supplement A)' so readers can assess claims.

code_availability.notes: No formal code availability statement. The paper describes prompts, skills, agent definitions, and verifier contracts as artefacts intended for publication as method ('Such scaffolding is worth publishing as method', Abstract).

author_contributions.notes: Author list not final ('co-author(s) TBC' on title page); Table 10 caption attributes evidence-collection contributions to BBS and SAR.

ethics_approval.notes: Not applicable: no human or animal subjects; study analyses LLM outputs and the authors' own research workflows.

permits_and_authorizations.notes: Not applicable to this meta-research study.

preregistration.notes: Not preregistered. Second episode described as arising incidentally during manuscript preparation (Section 5.3).

items.name: Supplement A: Extended 2025 findings
items.description: Referenced throughout: per-tool results, prompt lineage (A.3), transcripts (A.2), error catalogue and per-model comparison (A.5), accuracy comparison and per-model FAIMS outputs (A.4), release/availability timeline. Included in the assembled draft PDF.

supplementary_materials.notes: Supplement A is bound into the draft build; deposit location and PIDs to be settled at submission.

references_completeness.assessment: Bibliography builds from paper-b.bib and section2-grounding.bib via biber; references render in the draft. Formal completeness check deferred to submission QA.

fair_assessment.notes: FAIR scoring deliberately deferred: pre-submission draft with repositories and DOIs not final. To be scored post-submission if desired.

extraction_metadata.notes: Light-mode Pass 6 per user instruction: infrastructure recorded where present, absences documented as pre-submission expectations rather than transparency failures.
